dict.string:
Periodically reindexes all zk notebooks on this Mac so queries (via the zk CLI or zk-mcp) return current backlink/tag/FTS results.
Runs __HOME__/.local/bin/zk-reindex-all every 60 seconds (StartInterval), and once at user login (RunAtLoad). The script auto-discovers .zk/ directories under $HOME with smart exclusions (Library, media libraries, VM disks, container caches, package-manager caches). New notebooks created via `zk init` anywhere under the scan roots are picked up on the next cycle — no service restart needed.
Cost: typical run is <1s wall time and ~zero SSD writes when nothing changed (zk uses mtime+size checks before re-parsing). Comparable to other always-on macOS background work (Spotlight, Time Machine, cloud sync).
Configuration via env vars (set in EnvironmentVariables below or shell): ZK_BIN Path to zk binary (default: looks on PATH) ZK_REINDEX_ROOTS Colon-separated scan roots (default: $HOME) ZK_REINDEX_EXCLUDES Colon-separated extra paths to prune
Logs: __HOME__/Library/Logs/zk-reindex-all.log Source: https://github.com/johntrandall/zk-reindex-launchd
